---
title: "Creating A Governance Project"
---

---
title: "Creating a Governance Project"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Governance/wuo1747310776994"
section: "Hyland Governance"
---

Creating a Governance project involves creating a configuration that includes a file plan, a category, and a retention policy. After the Governance project is created, you can add more file plans and categories. Each category has to have one retention policy to enable deploying the project.

To create a Governance project:

1.  Access Studio Modeler. For more information on Studio Modeler, see the Hyland Automate Modeler documentation.
2.  Click All Governance projects located on the left pane in the  Governance Configuration section.
3.  Click the Create Project button located on the upper right corner. The Create Governance Project window is displayed. ![Create Governance Project window](data:image/png;base64,)
4.  Enter a unique project name in theProject Name field.  Note: The project name in the Project Name field can't exceed 100 characters.
5.  Optional: Enter a description in the Project description field.
6.  Click Create Project. The Governance project site containing File Plan Categories is displayed.
When creating a project, a file plan with the project name is created automatically. If you want to create another file plan for this project, see Creating a File Plan

7.  When the Governance project and file plan are created you can create a category. To create a category, see Creating a Category
